    <!-- Snippet: legacy-headEnd -->
<meta name="color-scheme" content="dark">
<style data-override-sandpack>
/* For iOS: prevent browser zoom when clicking on sandbox.
   Does NOT apply to code blocks. */
@supports (-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch) {
  @media screen and (max-width: 768px) {
    .cm-content {
      font-size: inherit !important;
    }
  }
}
</style>
<style>
body {
  --black: #0f1115;
  --white: #f5f5f5;
  --yellow: #fff312;
  --hue-0: var(--yellow);
  --hue-1: #ff0088;
  --hue-2: #dd00ee;
  --hue-3: #9911ff;
  --hue-4: #0d63f8;
  --hue-5: #0cdcf7;
  --hue-6: #4ff0b7;

  --hue-0-transparent: #fff31244;
  --hue-1-transparent: #ff008844;
  --hue-2-transparent: #dd00ee44;
  --hue-3-transparent: #9911ff44;
  --hue-4-transparent: #0d63f844;
  --hue-5-transparent: #0cdcf744;
  --hue-6-transparent: #4ff0b744;

    --accent: var(--hue-0);
    --accent-transparent: var(--hue-0-transparent);
}

html, .cm-scroller {
    scrollbar-color: var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, rgb(235, 235, 235)) transparent;
}

.cm-scroller {
    scrollbar-width: thin;
}

blockquote p {
  z-index: 1;
  position: relative;
}

blockquote .framer-text {
  margin-top: 20px !important;
}

blockquote::after {
  content: "â";
  color: var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  position: absolute;
  left: 0;
  top: 0;
  border-radius: 50%;
  width: 56px;
  height: 200px;
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  font-size: 300px;
  line-height: 200px;
}

.spring-visualiser {
  padding: 20px;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  border-radius: 10px;
  background-color: var(--token-74b02f5b-961b-49eb-8e13-4477bd831f3e, #12141a);
}

.spring-visualiser .select-container {
  position: relative;
}

.spring-visualiser .select-container::before  {
  --size: 5px;
  position: absolute;
  content: "";
  right: 18px;
  pointer-events: none;
}

.spring-visualiser .select-container::before {
  border-left: var(--size) solid transparent;
  border-right: var(--size) solid transparent;
  border-top: var(--size) solid var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  top: 18px;
}

.spring-visualiser label {
  color: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  font-family: Gabarito;
  font-size: 16px;
  gap: 5px;
  margin: 10px 0;
}

.spring-visualiser input, .input {
  color: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  font-family: "Azeret Mono", monospace;
  font-size: 14px;
}

.spring-visualiser select, .select {
  appearance: none;
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  width: 100%;
  font-family: Gabarito;
  font-size: 16px;
  padding: 10px;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  border-radius: 5px;
  cursor: pointer;
  color: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=number]:focus-visible,
.spring-visualiser select:focus-visible {
  outline: 1px solid rgb(79, 240, 183);
}

.input[type=number]:focus-visible,
.select:focus-visible {
  outline: 1px solid var(--token-65b54c24-8db9-454b-9fc2-5de614e025ca, rgb(255, 43, 110));
}

.spring-visualiser .inputs {
  display: flex;
  gap: 5px;
  padding-top: 5px;
}

.spring-visualiser .inputs input[type=number], .input[type=number] {
  appearance: none;
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  font-family: "Azeret Mono", monospace;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  border-radius: 5px;
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
  color: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  padding: 7px 9px;
  width: 80px;
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=range], .input[type=range] {
  -webkit-appearance: none; 
  width: 200px;
  background: transparent;
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-thumb, .input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-thumb {
  -webkit-appearance: none;
}

.spring-visualiser > div:nth-child(2) {
  padding: 40px 0 0 0!important;
}

/* Webkit */
.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-thumb, .input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-thumb {
  -webkit-appearance: none;
  width: 16px;
  height: 16px;
  background: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  border-radius: 50%;
  cursor: grab;
  margin-top: -5.5px;
}

/* All the same stuff for Firefox */
.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-moz-range-thumb, .input[type=range]::-moz-range-thumb {
  width: 16px;
  height: 16px;
  background: var(--framer-text-color, #fff);
  border-radius: 50%;
  cursor: grab;
}

/* Track styles */
.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-runnable-track, .input[type=range]::-webkit-slider-runnable-track {
  width: 100%;
  height: 8px;
  cursor: pointer;
  border-radius: 2px;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
  transition: background-color 0.2s ease-out;
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=range]:focus::-webkit-slider-runnable-track {
 background-color: rgb(79, 240, 183);
}

.input[type=range]:focus::-webkit-slider-runnable-track {
  background-color: var(--token-65b54c24-8db9-454b-9fc2-5de614e025ca, rgb(255, 43, 110));
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=range]::-moz-range-track, .input[type=range]::-moz-range-track {
  width: 100%;
  height: 8px;
  cursor: pointer;
  border-radius: 2px;
  border: 1px solid var(--token-6665bf47-c092-461e-b1a2-a1332f6b6ec5, #2e3542);
  background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.03);
}

.spring-visualiser label.use-visual-duration {
  flex-direction: row;
  display: flex;
}

.spring-visualiser input[type=checkbox] {
  accent-color: rgb(79, 240, 183);
}

.input[type=checkbox] {
  accent-color: var(--token-65b54c24-8db9-454b-9fc2-5de614e025ca, rgb(255, 43, 110));
}

.__framer-search-modal-container > div:first-child {
  background-color: transparent !important;
  background-image: radial-gradient(transparent 1px, #000 1px) !important;
  background-size: 4px 4px !important;
  backdrop-filter: brightness(1) blur(10px);
}

::selection {
  color: var(--white);
  background: var(--hue-4);
}
</style>

<!-- SnippetEnd: legacy-headEnd -->

    <!-- End of headEnd -->
</head>

data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><h1 class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1797nx5" data-styles-preset="XpCCubdWP">Motion component</h1></div></header><div class="ssr-variant hidden-10pzhmj hidden-1i9x81p"><div class="framer-a4hymo" data-framer-name="Content" style="transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Most <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animation">React animations</a><!--/$--> in Motion are powered by the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">&lt;motion /&gt;</code> component.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">There's a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> component for every HTML and SVG element, for instance <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.div</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.circle</code> etc. It extends standard React components with animation props that run at up to 120fps - without triggering React re-renders.</p><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="usage" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#usage" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Usage</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Import <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> from Motion:</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-qqho31">Building with AI? The <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1cmv7nt" href="./studio-ai-context">Motion Studio MCP</a><!--/$--> gives your AI editor access to the latest docs and source code of 330+ examples.</p></div></div></div></div><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-519g1s" id="performance" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#performance" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Performance</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components bypass React's render cycle entirely. Animated values update on every frame via the browser's native animation pipeline, so even complex animations with dozens of animated properties won't cause React re-renders or style/layout thrashing.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Using <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-motion-value">motion values</a><!--/$--> instead of React state to update <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">style</code> will also avoid re-renders.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" components</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">You can add motion capabilities to any React component with <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.create()</code>. The returned component accepts all standard motion props (<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">drag</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layout</code>, etc.) dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, all <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> props (like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> etc) are filtered out of the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">props</code> forwarded to the provided component. By providing a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">forwardMotionProps</code> config, the provided component will receive these props.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Pan and drag events are provided the origin <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> as well as an object <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code> that contains <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> point values for the following:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">point</code>: Relative to the device or page.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">delta</code>: Distance since the last event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">offset</code>: Distance from the original event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">velocity</code>: Current velocity of the pointer.</p></li></ul><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="onpanstart" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#onpanstart" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onPanStart</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when a pan gesture starts. Provided the triggering <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code>.</p><div class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="dragelastic" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragelastic" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragElastic</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">The degree of movement allowed outside constraints. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> = no movement, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> = full movement.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Set to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code> by default. Can also be set as <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> to disable movement.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By passing an object of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">top</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">right</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">bottom</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">left</code>, individual values can be set per constraint. Any missing values will be set to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) /* {&quot;name&quot;:&quot;Main Card&quot;} dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="dragdirectionlock" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragdirectionlock" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragDirectionLock</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Locks drag direction into the soonest detected direction. For example, if the component is moved more on the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> axis than <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> axis before the drag gesture kicks in, it will <strong class="framer-text">only</strong> drag on the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> axis for the remainder of the gesture.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="dragcontrols" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragcontrols" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Usually, dragging is initiated by pressing down on a component and moving it. For some use-cases, for instance clicking at an arbitrary point on a video scrubber, we might want to initiate dragging from a different component than the draggable one.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By creating a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code> using the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-drag-controls">useDragControls</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-drag-controls"> hook</a><!--/$-->, we can pass this into the draggable component's <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code> prop. It exposes a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">start</code> method that can start dragging from pointer events on other components.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) class="framer-vb9j5w"><div class="framer-pwf8a9" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-9913619f-76ec-4fff-b5d9-aef25028820c, rgb(254, 246, 42));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-qqho31">Given that by setting <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">dragControls</code> you are taking control of initiating the drag gesture, it is possible to disable the draggable element as the initiator by setting <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">dragListener={false}</code>.</p></div></div></div></div><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="draglistener" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#draglistener" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragListener</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Determines whether to trigger the drag gesture from event listeners. If passing <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code>, setting this to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> will ensure dragging can only be initiated by the controls, rather than a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">pointerdown</code> event on the draggable element.</p><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="ondrag" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#ondrag" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onDrag</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when the drag gesture is recognised on this element.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Pan and drag events are provided the origin <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> as well as an object <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code> that contains <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> point values for the following:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">point</code>: Relative to the device or page.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">delta</code>: Distance since the last event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">offset</code>: Distance from the original event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">velocity</code>: Current velocity of the pointer.</p></li></ul><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="ondragstart" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#ondragstart" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onDragStart</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when a drag gesture starts. Provided the triggering <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code>.</p><div class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Available options:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">once</code>: If <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">true</code>, once element enters the viewport it won't detect subsequent leave/enter events.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">root</code>: The <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">ref</code> of an ancestor scrollable element to detect intersections with (instead of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">window</code>).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">margin</code>: A margin to add to the viewport to change the detection area. Defaults to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px"</code>. Use multiple values to adjust top/right/bottom/left, e.g. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px -20px 0px 100px"</code>.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">amount</code>: The amount of an element that should enter the viewport to be considered "entered". Either <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"some"</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"all"</code> or a number between <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code>. Defaults to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"some"</code>.</p></li></ul><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="onviewportenter" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#onviewportenter" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onViewportEnter</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when an element enters the viewport. dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">If the previous component remains in the tree, the two elements will crossfade.</p><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="layoutdependency" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#layoutdependency" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutDependency</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, layout changes are detected every render. To reduce measurements and thus improve performance, you can pass a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutDependency</code> prop. Measurements will only occur when this value changes.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-519g1s" id="layoutanchor" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#layoutanchor" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutAnchor</code></a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">{ x: 0, y: 0 }</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion's layout animations look correct when a parent and child animate with different transitions, because it resolves the child's position relative to its parent.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, it does this using the top/left of the parent. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutAnchor</code> can customise this point, where <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> can be set as independent progress values between <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code>.</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> = top/left</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code> = center</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> = bottom/right</p></li></ul><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Setting to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> disables relative projection for this element, and elements will animate relative to their page-relative change.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">For layout animations to work correctly within scrollable elements, their scroll offset needs measuring. For performance reasons, Framer Motion doesn't measure the scroll offset of every ancestor. Add the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutScroll</code> prop to elements that should be measured.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="onlayoutanimationstart" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#onlayoutanimationstart" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onLayoutAnimationStart</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">A callback to run when a layout animation starts.</p><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="onlayoutanimationcomplete" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#onlayoutanimationcomplete" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onLayoutAnimationComplete</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">A callback to run when a layout animation completes.</p><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-519g1s" id="advanced" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#advanced" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Advanced</a></h3><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="inherit" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#inherit" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">inherit</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Set to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> to prevent a component inheriting or propagating changes in a parent variant.</p><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-741bi3" id="custom" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#custom" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transformTemplate</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, transforms are applied in order of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">translate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">scale</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">rotate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">skew</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">To change this, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transformTemplate</code> can be set as a function that accepts the latest transforms and the generated transform string and returns a new transform string.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="faqs" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#faqs" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">FAQs</a></h2><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cxO6A framer-1u5zllm framer-v-1u5zllm" data-framer-name="Variant 1"><div class="framer-1ononz8"><div class="framer-15gjlm3-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-pi52em" data-framer-name="Open" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">What is the &lt;motion /&gt; component?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:1;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">&lt;motion /&gt; is a drop-in replacement for HTML and SVG elements that adds animation capabilities. Instead of writing &lt;div&gt;, you write &lt;motion.div&gt;. The element behaves identically but can now accept animation props like animate, whileHover, and transition.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:rotate(45deg)" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-6nzm0c-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">How do I animate an element in React with Motion?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">Pass an animate prop to any &lt;motion /&gt; component with the values you want to animate to. For example, &lt;motion.div animate={{ opacity: 1 }} /&gt; will animate opacity from its current value to 1. Motion automatically detects changes and animates between them.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-17edq8i-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">Does &lt;motion /&gt; affect performance?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">&lt;motion /&gt; is optimised to animate transform and opacity on the compositor thread wherever possible, avoiding layout and paint. For best performance, prefer animating transform and opacity over properties like width or top.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-1jbs2gk-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">Can I use &lt;motion /&gt; with custom components?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">Yes. Use motion.create() to wrap any component that forwards its ref and accepts a style prop. For example: const MotionButton = motion.create(Button).</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-hnct4x-container"><!--$--><script type="application/ld+json">{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is the <motion /> component?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "<motion /> is a drop-in replacement for HTML and SVG elements that adds animation capabilities. Instead of writing <div>, you write <motion.div>. The element behaves identically but can now accept animation props like animate, whileHover, and transition."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I animate an element in React with Motion?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Pass an animate prop to any <motion /> component with the values you want to animate to. For example, <motion.div animate={{ opacity: 1 }} /> will animate opacity from its current value to 1. Motion automatically detects changes and animates between them."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Does <motion /> affect performance?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "<motion /> is optimised to animate transform and opacity on the compositor thread wherever possible, avoiding layout and paint. For best performance, prefer animating transform and opacity over properties like width or top."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use <motion /> with custom components?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. Use motion.create() to wrap any component that forwards its ref and accepts a style prop. For example: const MotionButton = motion.create(Button)."
      }
    }
  ]
}</script><!--/$--></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="ssr-variant hidden-uq7ul hidden-1i9x81p"><div class="framer-a4hymo" data-framer-name="Content" style="transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Most <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animation">React animations</a><!--/$--> in Motion are powered by the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">&lt;motion /&gt;</code> component.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">There's a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> component for every HTML and SVG element, for instance <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.div</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.circle</code> etc. It extends standard React components with animation props that run at up to 120fps - without triggering React re-renders.</p><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="usage" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#usage" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Usage</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Import <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code>   <span class="sp-syntax-comment">// Animate when this value changes:</span>
  <span class="sp-syntax-property">animate</span>=<span class="sp-syntax-punctuation">{</span><span class="sp-syntax-punctuation">{</span> <span class="sp-syntax-property">scale</span><span class="sp-syntax-punctuation">:</span> <span class="sp-syntax-static">2</span> <span class="sp-syntax-punctuation">}</span><span class="sp-syntax-punctuation">}</span>

  <span class="sp-syntax-comment">// Animate the component when its layout changes:</span>

data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-qqho31">Building with AI? The <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1cmv7nt" href="./studio-ai-context">Motion Studio MCP</a><!--/$--> gives your AI editor access to the latest docs and source code of 330+ examples.</p></div></div></div></div><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="performance" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#performance" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Performance</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components bypass React's render cycle entirely. Animated values update on every frame via the browser's native animation pipeline, so even complex animations with dozens of animated properties won't cause React re-renders or style/layout thrashing.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Using <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-motion-value">motion values</a><!--/$--> instead of React state to update <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">style</code> will also avoid re-renders.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" components</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">You can add motion capabilities to any React component with <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.create()</code>. The returned component accepts all standard motion props (<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">drag</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layout</code>, etc.) alongside the original component's props.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" This will make a new component every render, breaking your animations.</p></div></div></div></div><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">It's also possible to pass strings to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.create</code>, which will create custom DOM elements.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, all <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> props (like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> etc) are filtered out of the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">props</code> forwarded to the provided component. By providing a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">forwardMotionProps</code> config, the provided component will receive these props.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Pan and drag events are provided the origin <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> as well as an object <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code> that contains <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> point values for the following:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">point</code>: Relative to the device or page.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">delta</code>: Distance since the last event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">offset</code>: Distance from the original event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">velocity</code>: Current velocity of the pointer.</p></li></ul><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="onpanstart" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#onpanstart" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onPanStart</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when a pan gesture starts. Provided the triggering <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code>.</p><div class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="dragelastic" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragelastic" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragElastic</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">The degree of movement allowed outside constraints. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> = no movement, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> = full movement.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Set to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code> by default. Can also be set as <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> to disable movement.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By passing an object of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">top</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">right</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">bottom</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">left</code>, individual values can be set per constraint. Any missing values will be set to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="dragdirectionlock" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragdirectionlock" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragDirectionLock</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Locks drag direction into the soonest detected direction. For example, if the component is moved more on the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> axis than <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> axis before the drag gesture kicks in, it will <strong class="framer-text">only</strong> drag on the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> axis for the remainder of the gesture.</p><div dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="dragcontrols" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragcontrols" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Usually, dragging is initiated by pressing down on a component and moving it. For some use-cases, for instance clicking at an arbitrary point on a video scrubber, we might want to initiate dragging from a different component than the draggable one.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By creating a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code> using the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-drag-controls">useDragControls</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-drag-controls"> hook</a><!--/$-->, we can pass this into the draggable component's <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code> prop. It exposes a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">start</code> method that can start dragging from pointer events on other components.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) /* {&quot;name&quot;:&quot;Main Card&quot;} data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-qqho31">Given that by setting <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">dragControls</code> you are taking control of initiating the drag gesture, it is possible to disable the draggable element as the initiator by setting <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">dragListener={false}</code>.</p></div></div></div></div><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="draglistener" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#draglistener" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragListener</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Determines whether to trigger the drag gesture from event listeners. If passing <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code>, setting this to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> will ensure dragging can only be initiated by the controls, rather than a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">pointerdown</code> event on the draggable element.</p><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="ondrag" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#ondrag" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onDrag</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when the drag gesture is recognised on this element.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Pan and drag events are provided the origin <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> as well as an object <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code> that contains <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> point values for the following:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">point</code>: Relative to the device or page.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">delta</code>: Distance since the last event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">offset</code>: Distance from the original event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">velocity</code>: Current velocity of the pointer.</p></li></ul><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="ondragstart" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#ondragstart" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onDragStart</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when a drag gesture starts. Provided the triggering <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code>.</p><div class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Available options:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">once</code>: If <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">true</code>, once element enters the viewport it won't detect subsequent leave/enter events.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">root</code>: The <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">ref</code> of an ancestor scrollable element to detect intersections with (instead of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">window</code>).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">margin</code>: A margin to add to the viewport to change the detection area. Defaults to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px"</code>. Use multiple values to adjust top/right/bottom/left, e.g. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px -20px 0px 100px"</code>.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">amount</code>: The amount of an element that should enter the viewport to be considered "entered". Either <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"some"</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"all"</code> or a number between <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code>. Defaults to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"some"</code>.</p></li></ul><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="onviewportenter" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#onviewportenter" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onViewportEnter</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when an element enters the viewport. Provided the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/IntersectionObserverEntry" target="_blank" rel="noopener">IntersectionObserverEntry</a><!--/$--></code> with details of the intersection dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">If the previous component remains in the tree, the two elements will crossfade.</p><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="layoutdependency" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#layoutdependency" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutDependency</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, layout changes are detected every render. To reduce measurements and thus improve performance, you can pass a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutDependency</code> prop. Measurements will only occur when this value changes.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="layoutanchor" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#layoutanchor" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutAnchor</code></a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">{ x: 0, y: 0 }</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion's layout animations look correct when a parent and child animate with different transitions, because it resolves the child's position relative to its parent.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, it does this using the top/left of the parent. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutAnchor</code> can customise this point, where <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> can be set as independent progress values between <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code>.</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> = top/left</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code> = center</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> = bottom/right</p></li></ul><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Setting to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> disables relative projection for this element, and elements will animate relative to their page-relative change.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) /* {&quot;name&quot;:&quot;Main Card&quot;} style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#layoutscroll" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutScroll</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">For layout animations to work correctly within scrollable elements, their scroll offset needs measuring. For performance reasons, Framer Motion doesn't measure the scroll offset of every ancestor. Add the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutScroll</code> prop to elements that should be measured.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="transformtemplate" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#transformtemplate" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transformTemplate</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, transforms are applied in order of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">translate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">scale</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">rotate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">skew</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">To change this, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transformTemplate</code> can be set as a function that accepts the latest transforms and the generated transform string and returns a new transform string.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, 245, 245)))">What is the &lt;motion /&gt; component?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:1;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">&lt;motion /&gt; is a drop-in replacement for HTML and SVG elements that adds animation capabilities. Instead of writing &lt;div&gt;, you write &lt;motion.div&gt;. The element behaves identically but can now accept animation props like animate, whileHover, and transition.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:rotate(45deg)" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-6nzm0c-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">How do I animate an element in React with Motion?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">Pass an animate prop to any &lt;motion /&gt; component with the values you want to animate to. For example, &lt;motion.div animate={{ opacity: 1 }} /&gt; will animate opacity from its current value to 1. Motion automatically detects changes and animates between them.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-17edq8i-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">Does &lt;motion /&gt; affect performance?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">&lt;motion /&gt; is optimised to animate transform and opacity on the compositor thread wherever possible, avoiding layout and paint. For best performance, prefer animating transform and opacity over properties like width or top.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-1jbs2gk-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">Can I use &lt;motion /&gt; with custom components?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">Yes. Use motion.create() to wrap any component that forwards its ref and accepts a style prop. For example: const MotionButton = motion.create(Button).</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-hnct4x-container"><!--$--><script type="application/ld+json">{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is the <motion /> component?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "<motion /> is a drop-in replacement for HTML and SVG elements that adds animation capabilities. Instead of writing <div>, you write <motion.div>. The element behaves identically but can now accept animation props like animate, whileHover, and transition."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I animate an element in React with Motion?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Pass an animate prop to any <motion /> component with the values you want to animate to. For example, <motion.div animate={{ opacity: 1 }} /> will animate opacity from its current value to 1. Motion automatically detects changes and animates between them."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Does <motion /> affect performance?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "<motion /> is optimised to animate transform and opacity on the compositor thread wherever possible, avoiding layout and paint. For best performance, prefer animating transform and opacity over properties like width or top."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use <motion /> with custom components?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. Use motion.create() to wrap any component that forwards its ref and accepts a style prop. For example: const MotionButton = motion.create(Button)."
      }
    }
  ]
}</script><!--/$--></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="ssr-variant hidden-10pzhmj hidden-uq7ul"><div class="framer-a4hymo" data-framer-name="Content" style="transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Most <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-animation">React animations</a><!--/$--> in Motion are powered by the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">&lt;motion /&gt;</code> component.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">There's a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> component for every HTML and SVG element, for instance <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.div</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.circle</code> etc. It extends standard React components with animation props that run at up to 120fps - without triggering React re-renders.</p><h2 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="usage" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#usage" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Usage</a></h2><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Import <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-qqho31">Building with AI? The <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1cmv7nt" href="./studio-ai-context">Motion Studio MCP</a><!--/$--> gives your AI editor access to the latest docs and source code of 330+ examples.</p></div></div></div></div><h3 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="performance" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#performance" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">Performance</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> components bypass React's render cycle entirely. Animated values update on every frame via the browser's native animation pipeline, so even complex animations with dozens of animated properties won't cause React re-renders or style/layout thrashing.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Using <!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-motion-value">motion values</a><!--/$--> instead of React state to update <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">style</code> will also avoid re-renders.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" components</a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">You can add motion capabilities to any React component with <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion.create()</code>. The returned component accepts all standard motion props (<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">whileHover</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">drag</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layout</code>, etc.) alongside the original component's props.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, all <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">motion</code> props (like <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">animate</code> etc) are filtered out of the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">props</code> forwarded to the provided component. By providing a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">forwardMotionProps</code> config, the provided component will receive these props.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Pan and drag events are provided the origin <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> as well as an object <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code> that contains <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> point values for the following:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">point</code>: Relative to the device or page.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">delta</code>: Distance since the last event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">offset</code>: Distance from the original event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">velocity</code>: Current velocity of the pointer.</p></li></ul><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="onpanstart" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#onpanstart" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onPanStart</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when a pan gesture starts. Provided the triggering <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code>.</p><div class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="dragelastic" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragelastic" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragElastic</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">The degree of movement allowed outside constraints. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> = no movement, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> = full movement.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Set to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code> by default. Can also be set as <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> to disable movement.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By passing an object of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">top</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">right</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">bottom</code>/<code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">left</code>, individual values can be set per constraint. Any missing values will be set to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code>.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="dragdirectionlock" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragdirectionlock" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragDirectionLock</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Locks drag direction into the soonest detected direction. For example, if the component is moved more on the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> axis than <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> axis before the drag gesture kicks in, it will <strong class="framer-text">only</strong> drag on the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> axis for the remainder of the gesture.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="dragcontrols" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#dragcontrols" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Usually, dragging is initiated by pressing down on a component and moving it. For some use-cases, for instance clicking at an arbitrary point on a video scrubber, we might want to initiate dragging from a different component than the draggable one.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By creating a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code> using the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-drag-controls">useDragControls</a><!--/$--></code><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="./react-use-drag-controls"> hook</a><!--/$-->, we can pass this into the draggable component's <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code> prop. It exposes a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">start</code> method that can start dragging from pointer events on other components.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18))
