data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-qqho31">Given that by setting <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">dragControls</code> you are taking control of initiating the drag gesture, it is possible to disable the draggable element as the initiator by setting <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-k253on">dragListener={false}</code>.</p></div></div></div></div><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="draglistener" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#draglistener" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragListener</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Determines whether to trigger the drag gesture from event listeners. If passing <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">dragControls</code>, setting this to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> will ensure dragging can only be initiated by the controls, rather than a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">pointerdown</code> event on the draggable element.</p><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Pan and drag events are provided the origin <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">PointerEvent</code> as well as an object <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">info</code> that contains <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> point values for the following:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">point</code>: Relative to the device or page.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">delta</code>: Distance since the last event.</p></li><li from the original event.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Available options:</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">once</code>: If <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">true</code>, once element enters the viewport it won't detect subsequent leave/enter events.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">root</code>: The <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">ref</code> of an ancestor scrollable element to detect intersections with (instead of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">window</code>).</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">margin</code>: A margin to add to the viewport to change the detection area. Defaults to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px"</code>. Use multiple values to adjust top/right/bottom/left, e.g. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"0px -20px 0px 100px"</code>.</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">amount</code>: The amount of an element that should enter the viewport to be considered "entered". Either <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"some"</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"all"</code> or a number between <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code>. Defaults to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">"some"</code>.</p></li></ul><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="onviewportenter" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#onviewportenter" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">onViewportEnter</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Callback function that fires when an element enters the viewport. Provided the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam"><!--$--><a class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1kclasy" href="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/IntersectionObserverEntry" target="_blank" rel="noopener">IntersectionObserverEntry</a><!--/$--></code> with details of the intersection event.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">If the previous component remains in the tree, the two elements will crossfade.</p><h4 dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="layoutdependency" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#layoutdependency" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutDependency</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, layout changes are detected every render. To reduce measurements and thus improve performance, you can pass a <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutDependency</code> prop. Measurements will only occur when this value changes.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-6lndsa" id="layoutanchor" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#layoutanchor" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutAnchor</code></a></h3><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><strong class="framer-text">Default: </strong><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">{ x: 0, y: 0 }</code></p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Motion's layout animations look correct when a parent and child animate with different transitions, because it resolves the child's position relative to its parent.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, it does this using the top/left of the parent. <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutAnchor</code> can customise this point, where <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">x</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">y</code> can be set as independent progress values between <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code>.</p><ul dir="auto" class="framer-text"><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0</code> = top/left</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">0.5</code> = center</p></li><li data-preset-tag="p" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">1</code> = bottom/right</p></li></ul><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">Setting to <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">false</code> disables relative projection for this element, and elements will animate relative to their page-relative change.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18)) /* {&quot;name&quot;:&quot;Main Card&quot;} style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#layoutscroll" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutScroll</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">For layout animations to work correctly within scrollable elements, their scroll offset needs measuring. For performance reasons, Framer Motion doesn't measure the scroll offset of every ancestor. Add the <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutScroll</code> prop to elements that should be measured.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">layoutRoot</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">For layout animations to work correctly within <code dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-2u0z3l" id="transformtemplate" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#transformtemplate" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0"><code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transformTemplate</code></a></h4><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">By default, transforms are applied in order of <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">translate</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">scale</code>, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">rotate</code> and <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">skew</code>.</p><p dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-8ohns9">To change this, <code class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1x44pam">transformTemplate</code> can be set as a function that accepts the latest transforms and the generated transform string and returns a new transform string.</p><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cb" style="--cb-colors-surface1-light:var(--cb-custom-background, dir="auto" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-exngks" id="faqs" style="scroll-margin-top:150px"><a href="#faqs" class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1p15ko0">FAQs</a></h2><div class="framer-text framer-text-module" style="width:100%;height:auto" data-width="fill"><div class="framer-cxO6A framer-1u5zllm framer-v-1u5zllm" data-framer-name="Variant 1"><div class="framer-1ononz8"><div class="framer-15gjlm3-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-pi52em" data-framer-name="Open" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">What is the &lt;motion /&gt; component?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:1;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">&lt;motion /&gt; is a drop-in replacement for HTML and SVG elements that adds animation capabilities. Instead of writing &lt;div&gt;, you write &lt;motion.div&gt;. The element behaves identically but can now accept animation props like animate, whileHover, and transition.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:rotate(45deg)" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-6nzm0c-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">How do I animate an element in React with Motion?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">Pass an animate prop to any &lt;motion /&gt; component with the values you want to animate to. For example, &lt;motion.div animate={{ opacity: 1 }} /&gt; will animate opacity from its current value to 1. Motion automatically detects changes and animates between them.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-17edq8i-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">Does &lt;motion /&gt; affect performance?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">&lt;motion /&gt; is optimised to animate transform and opacity on the compositor thread wherever possible, avoiding layout and paint. For best performance, prefer animating transform and opacity over properties like width or top.</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-1jbs2gk-container"><div class="framer-3yyhF framer-sAPsb framer-pi52em framer-v-1x6yx0m" data-framer-name="Closed" data-highlight="true" style="background-color:var(--token-370011fe-f455-4f36-bd1d-72d4dac0be54, rgb(18, 18, 18));width:100%;border-bottom-left-radius:10px;border-bottom-right-radius:10px;border-top-left-radius:10px;border-top-right-radius:10px" tabindex="0"><div class="framer-13x1h8o"><div class="framer-1k5ojjv" style="--extracted-r6o4lv:var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245));transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto" style="--framer-text-color:var(--extracted-r6o4lv, var(--token-d47092a9-9a47-4652-ac9a-ad62db14e073, rgb(245, 245, 245)))">Can I use &lt;motion /&gt; with custom components?</p></div><div class="framer-66ghph" style="opacity:0;transform:none" data-framer-component-type="RichTextContainer"><p class="framer-text framer-styles-preset-1qzj7jy" data-styles-preset="OCes52Yrx" dir="auto">Yes. Use motion.create() to wrap any component that forwards its ref and accepts a style prop. For example: const MotionButton = motion.create(Button).</p></div></div><div data-framer-component-type="SVG" data-framer-name="Plus" style="image-rendering:pixelated;flex-shrink:0;fill:rgb(255, 255, 255);color:rgb(255, 255, 255);transform:none" class="framer-1h7oqqg" aria-hidden="true"><div class="svgContainer" style="width:100%;height:100%;aspect-ratio:inherit"><svg style="width:100%;height:100%;" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><use href="#svg-496994454_224"/></svg></div></div></div></div><div class="framer-hnct4x-container"><!--$--><script type="application/ld+json">{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "FAQPage",
  "mainEntity": [
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "What is the <motion /> component?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "<motion /> is a drop-in replacement for HTML and SVG elements that adds animation capabilities. Instead of writing <div>, you write <motion.div>. The element behaves identically but can now accept animation props like animate, whileHover, and transition."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "How do I animate an element in React with Motion?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Pass an animate prop to any <motion /> component with the values you want to animate to. For example, <motion.div animate={{ opacity: 1 }} /> will animate opacity from its current value to 1. Motion automatically detects changes and animates between them."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Does <motion /> affect performance?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "<motion /> is optimised to animate transform and opacity on the compositor thread wherever possible, avoiding layout and paint. For best performance, prefer animating transform and opacity over properties like width or top."
      }
    },
    {
      "@type": "Question",
      "name": "Can I use <motion /> with custom components?",
      "acceptedAnswer": {
        "@type": "Answer",
        "text": "Yes. Use motion.create() to wrap any component that forwards its ref and accepts a style prop. For example: const MotionButton = motion.create(Button)."
      }
    }
  ]

The element behaves identically but can now accept animation props like animate, whileHover, and transition.\",\"yNVSfLieZ\":\"How do I animate an element in React with Motion?\",\"wfzV2tIeI\":\"Pass an animate prop to any \u003Cmotion /> component with the values you want to animate to. For example, \u003Cmotion.div animate={{ opacity: 1 }} /> will animate opacity from its current value to 1. Motion automatically detects changes and animates between them.\",\"tkibv_FUY\":\"Does \u003Cmotion /> affect performance?\",\"N1H91Ealb\":\"\u003Cmotion /> is optimised to animate transform and opacity on the compositor thread wherever possible, avoiding layout and paint. transition.\"]],[4,\"div\",{\"className\":\"framer-text-module\",\"style\":{\"width\":\"100%\",\"height\":\"auto\"},\"data-width\":\"fill\"},[3,\"module:pVk4QsoHxASnVtUBp6jr/ttReBBOK4JjO79S16uu9/CodeBlock.js:default\",{\"code\":\"startTransition(() \"],[4,\"code\",null,[5,\"exit\"]],[5,\", \"],[4,\"code\",null,[5,\"share\"]],[5,\" and \"],[4,\"code\",null,[5,\"update\"]],[5,\" props can all resolve dynamically, with a list of values set via You can use this information to generate different animations.\"]],[4,\"div\",{\"className\":\"framer-text-module\",\"style\":{\"width\":\"100%\",\"height\":\"auto\"},\"data-width\":\"fill\"},[3,\"module:pVk4QsoHxASnVtUBp6jr/ttReBBOK4JjO79S16uu9/CodeBlock.js:default\",{\"code\":\"\u003CAnimateView\\n  key={index}\\n  exit={(types) => ({\\n      transform: `translateX(${types.includes(\\\"prev\\\") ? 100 : -100}%)`,\\n  })}\\n  enter={(types) => ({\\n      transform: [\\n          `translateX(${types.includes(\\\"next\\\") ? 100 : -100}%)`,\\n          \\\"translateX(0%)\\\",\\n      ],\\n  })}\\n>\",\"language\":\"JSX\"},[],[]]],[4,\"div\",{\"className\":\"framer-text-module\",\"style\":{\"width\":\"100%\",\"height\":\"auto\"},\"data-width\":\"fill\"},[3,\"module:05B5K0pCl9oc4uJEto2N/BJPpejUDWWaYUUJXIIag/Example.js:default\",{\"platform\":\"react\",\"id\":\"animate-view-types\"},[],[]]],[4,\"h3\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"Suspense\"]],[4,\"p\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[4,\"code\",null,[5,\"AnimateView\"]],[5,\" integrates with \"],[4,\"code\",null,[5,\"Suspense\"]],[5,\". You can crossfade between content and fallback by wrapping them both in \"],[4,\"code\",null,[5,\"Suspense\"]],[5,\":\"]],[4,\"div\",{\"className\":\"framer-text-module\",\"style\":{\"width\":\"100%\",\"height\":\"auto\"},\"data-width\":\"fill\"},[3,\"module:pVk4QsoHxASnVtUBp6jr/ttReBBOK4JjO79S16uu9/CodeBlock.js:default\",{\"code\":\"\u003CAnimateView>\\n  \u003CSuspense fallback={\u003CPlaceholder />}>\\n    \u003CContent />\\n  \u003C/Suspense>\\n\u003C/AnimateView>\",\"language\":\"JSX\"},[],[]]],[4,\"h2\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"Performance\"]],[4,\"p\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"The React docs state:\"]],[4,\"blockquote\",null,[4,\"p\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[4,\"code\",null,[5,\"\u003CViewTransition>\"]],[5,\" creates an image that can be moved around, scaled and cross-faded. Unlike Layout Animations you may have seen in React Native or Motion, this means that not every individual Element inside of it animates its position. This can lead to better performance and a more continuous feeling, smooth animation compared to animating every individual piece.\"]]],[4,\"p\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"Neither of these claims are true.\"]],[4,\"p\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"From our own stress test benchmarking, creating image bitmaps and constructing a pseudo-DOM is more memory intensive and slower than the equivalent layout measurements used by Motion's \"],[2,{\"href\":{\"webPageId\":\"MtVxH2FBo\",\"pathVariables\":{\"sg3kghrCF\":\"layout-animations\"},\"unresolvedPathSlugs\":{\"sg3kghrCF\":{\"collectionItemId\":\"aOgXxq81H\",\"collectionId\":\"AaN8oZOpy\"}}},\"openInNewTab\":false,\"smoothScroll\":false,\"relValues\":[],\"motionChild\":true,\"nodeId\":\"AaN8oZOpy\",\"scopeId\":\"contentManagement\"},[4,\"a\",null,[5,\"layout animations\"]]],[5,\".\"]],[4,\"p\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"The claim of \\\"a more continuous feeling\\\" is also not right. Layout animations are **\"],[4,\"strong\",null,[5,\"interruptible\"]],[5,\"**, which means you can change direction mid-animation and they respond immediately. View transitions are **\"],[4,\"strong\",null,[5,\"not interruptible\"]],[5,\"**, meaning they must complete before a new transition can begin. This makes layout animations a far better candidate for micro-interactions where responsiveness matters.\"]],[4,\"p\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"View transitions are best suited for **\"],[4,\"strong\",null,[5,\"page-level transitions\"]],[5,\"** (route changes, full-view swaps) where the non-interruptible nature is acceptable and the snapshot-based approach avoids complex per-element coordination.\"]],[4,\"h2\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"Props\"]],[4,\"h3\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[4,\"code\",null,[5,\"transition\"]]],[4,\"p\",{\"dir\":\"auto\"},[5,\"Default transition for all animation types. Accepts any Motion transition, including transition={{ type: spring, visualDuration: 0.4, bounce: 0.3 document.addEventListener('keydown', function(event) {
